_about: Stage-1 structural pre-scan allowlist for MCPS (security-audit-funnel). Each entry is a structural-floor exception confirmed JUSTIFIED from source on 2026-06-14. The pre-scan blocks on unsafe / security-path panics / noop-in-binary by design; these are the reviewed, intentional cases. Removing an entry re-surfaces the finding.

_justifications.unsafe: Every `unsafe` is OS/HSM FFI confined to dedicated integration files, each carrying a `// SAFETY:` justification at the call site: pkcs11_native.rs (cryptoki C bindings, 23/23 SAFETY-commented), inner_launch.rs (async-signal-safe close(2)/getrlimit in the post-fork child), persistent_inner.rs (fcntl O_NONBLOCK + poll on fds the proxy owns), rlimits.rs (setrlimit, async-signal-safe), sandbox_linux.rs (landlock/seccomp enforcement), cli.rs (libc::kill(SIGKILL) on the proxy's own un-reaped child from the watchdog thread). There is no non-FFI unsafe in the tree.
_justifications.panic: mcps-host/src/nonce.rs: getrandom(out).expect(...) is a DELIBERATE fail-closed on OS-CSPRNG unavailability — a host that cannot draw entropy must NOT emit a predictable nonce, so it fails loudly rather than degrade silently (documented at the call site). Not reachable from untrusted input; an environment failure, not an input failure.
_justifications.noop-in-binary: mcps-demo/src/bin/*: demonstration binaries wire InMemory* reference implementations to show the protocol end-to-end; they are not production. InMemoryRevocationSource (mcps-proxy/src/main.rs): the revocation source of the OPT-IN Reference Signed Authorization Profile (--authz reference; the proxy defaults to authz Off). mcps-policy/src/reference.rs documents this profile as explicitly NOT the long-term recommendation (Biscuit is); a production deployment supplies its own authorization profile. AUDIT NOTE: reference-profile authorization revocation is in-memory, non-persistent, and per-node (not shared) — call this out in the Stage-2/3 review and the security boundary, do not treat the allowlist as hiding it.
